$comment: Release self-declaration consumed by the unified registry's auto-core-release resolver (sh2-plugin-registry/scripts/resolve-core-candidate.mjs) at the git tag being released. The supports.* semver ranges decide whether a tagged core can be auto-matched with the latest published counterparts into a signed release candidate PR. Keep pluginApiVersion in sync with config/services.yaml (CI enforces it) and widen/bump supports.frontend deliberately - while the platform is pre-1.0, every 0.x minor is breaking. Raise supports.frontend's lower bound whenever core changes/removes an API contract the frontend depends on. Floor 0.1.30 is the first frontend that BOTH (a) adopts the field-naming unification - migration Version20260622165615 drops the `shared_` prefix from 47 style props (`shared_color`->`color`, `shared_variant`->`variant`, `shared_radius`->`radius`, ...; reserved `shared_height`/`shared_width`/`shared_icon` keep their prefix), so this core emits the unprefixed names a 0.1.23-0.1.28 frontend does not read - AND (b) handles the core 0.1.18 anonymous-access hardening: an anonymous `preview=true` is now rejected with 401, so frontend 0.1.30 gates SSR preview on a live session cookie and clears `sh_preview` on logout/expiry (mobile 0.1.10 ships the matching previewPolicy gate). A 0.1.29 frontend left a stale admin preview cookie 401-looping the public site against this core, so the floor moved 0.1.29 -> 0.1.30. The 0.1.30 floor still carries everything the previous floors required - the full 0.1.15 style-schema contract (mobile-rendering taxonomy: style `renderTarget`, required field `scope`, the unprefixed-common/web_/mobile_ field split with `pages.id_platform` removed in favour of the page access type) PLUS the alert/badge/avatar/button/login authoring-polish wave - so an older frontend can never be auto-assembled with this core into a release. The resolver refuses any incompatible frontend+backend pair in either direction. Core 0.1.20 adds the CMS-driven mobile-preview install/update endpoints (/cms-api/v1/admin/system/update/mobile-preview/*) and the `mobile_preview_version` field on /admin/system/version; both are ADDITIVE and optional, so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.30 (the core does not require the preview-update UI - frontend 0.1.32 adopts it and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.20). Core 0.1.21 adds the `admin.mobile_preview.view` permission (migration Version20260623193630) gating the frontend's full-screen Live Preview surface; it is ADDITIVE (no new route, no schema change - the live preview reuses the existing mint+exchange routes), so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.30 (frontend 0.1.33 adopts the live preview and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.21). Core 0.1.23 implements the immutable data-column contract (host issue #56): data_cols gains an immutable field_key + mutable display_name (+display_name_source), the admin columns endpoint now returns {id, fieldKey, displayName} instead of {id, name}, the interpolation variables move out of the getSection payload into a dedicated GET /cms-api/v1/admin/sections/{section_id}/data-variables endpoint (a token => label map), and a new PATCH /cms-api/v1/admin/data/tables/{tableName}/columns/display-name (permission admin.data.update_columns) curates labels. That is a BREAKING response-shape change the frontend consumes, so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.30 -> 0.1.48 (frontend 0.1.48 reads the new column shape + fetches the token/label picker endpoint and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.23). Core 0.1.24 is an ADDITIVE issue-#56 follow-up: renaming a CMS form input now propagates its name to the immutable section_<id> column's auto label on save; data_tables gains the same auto|manual provenance FK (id_display_name_source, migration Version20260629074004) with a new PATCH /cms-api/v1/admin/data/tables/{tableName}/display-name (permission admin.data.update_tables, migration Version20260629074552) to rename+lock a table; and a `locked` flag is added to the tables list + columns list while a form section's getSection payload gains a data_table {id,name,display_name,locked} block for the inspector badge/deep-link. All additions are backward-compatible extra fields + a new route, so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.48 (frontend 0.1.49 adopts the lock UI + deep link and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.24). Core 0.1.25 is the Interpolation v2 contract switch (host issue #56): the `{{ }}` editor and the render-time `retrieved_data` scope now resolve data variables by the immutable `field_key` instead of the mutable input name, so the section `data-variables` map becomes `{{scope.field_key}} => display_name` and a show-user-input section's payload gains a `field_labels` (`field_key` => `display_name`) header map with `entries` kept keyed by `field_key`. That is a BREAKING render/contract change the frontend consumes (label chips that store `{{field_key}}` + show-user-input headers that default to `display_name`), so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.48 -> 0.1.52 (frontend 0.1.52 adopts the chip/label round-trip + show-user-input header map and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.25). No data migration ships; pre-release `{{scope.name}}` content is recreated against the new field_key picker. Core 0.1.26 is an ADDITIVE Interpolation v2 follow-up: a single context-aware endpoint GET /cms-api/v1/admin/interpolation/variables (route admin_interpolation_variables_get, permission admin.page.read, migration Version20260629110606) now serves the `{{ }}` picker catalog for any context (section|page|action|global via InterpolationVariableService over DataVariableResolver's per-context catalogs), the seeded auth mail templates move onto the shared system.* namespace ({{system.user_name}}/{{system.user_code}} + one-time {{system.special.activation_link|reset_link|platform_link}}; MailTemplateService maps the flat caller vars onto that nested context, legacy flat tokens still resolve), and the action picker offers recipient.*/record.<field_key>/system.*. The new route is additive and the section data-variables route still exists (that legacy per-section route is later removed in core 0.1.29 once every supported frontend uses the unified endpoint), so an older frontend keeps working and supports.frontend stays >=0.1.52; frontend 0.1.53 adopts the unified endpoint across page/action/css/json surfaces and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.26. Core 0.1.27 is the type-driven-editor follow-up (host issue #56): the admin columns endpoint now PREPENDS the always-present projection columns (id/id_users/user_name/record_id/entry_date/id_users_deleted) flagged standard:true/id:null/locked:true, and migration Version20260629143116 retypes overloaded fields so the editor is chosen purely from the field type (7 structured-config blobs textarea->json; html_tag_content textarea->code; the short message fields error_text/empty_text/loading_text/confirm_message/delete_modal_body text->textarea). Both are response/behavior changes the frontend renders (a pre-0.1.54 frontend would mis-render the standard columns and the new `code` type), so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.52 -> 0.1.54 (frontend 0.1.54 renders the standard columns + the type-driven editors and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.27). Mail bodies also move to WYSIWYG rich-text fragments rendered at send time by MailHtmlRenderer (internal; no API contract change). Core 0.1.28 is a further type-driven follow-up (host issue #56): the free-form display fields `text` (shared by the text + highlight styles) and `blockquote_content` are retyped `markdown-inline -> textarea` (migration Version20260629153921) so admins author them in the full rich-text editor (Enter, headings, lists, links, alignment); the field catalog is also cleaned up (migration Version20260629150730 unlinks the `multi_select_data` duplicate from the `select` style and deletes the dead `web_combobox_data`/`items`/`labels` fields, plus enriches structured-field help examples). The matching frontend renders the BLOCK structure of those fields (TextStyle/BlockquoteStyle render the authored headings/lists instead of flattening them to inline), so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.54 -> 0.1.55 (frontend 0.1.55 renders the rich text/blockquote block content + ships the closable field-help popover with copyable JSON examples and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.28). The seeded auth mail bodies additionally drop the duplicated raw-URL callout (confirm/recovery) and the raw-token link text (password-changed) so the action link is carried by the styled button only (templates only; no API contract change). Core 0.1.29 removes the now-unused per-section interpolation-picker route GET /cms-api/v1/admin/sections/{section_id}/data-variables (migration Version20260629170535 drops the admin_sections_data_variables_get api_routes row + its admin.page.read link; the controller action, the AdminSectionService wrapper, and the section_data_variables response schema are deleted). It is fully superseded by the unified GET /cms-api/v1/admin/interpolation/variables endpoint that every frontend >=0.1.53 already uses, so the removal is floor-neutral and supports.frontend stays >=0.1.55. Core 0.1.30 is an internal interpolation-robustness fix (no API contract change): InterpolationService renders a non-scalar {{ }} token (most commonly {{system.user_group}}, a list of the user's groups) as an empty string instead of fataling htmlspecialchars() with a TypeError, and catches Throwable (not just Exception) around the Mustache render so a malformed token degrades to the original content rather than 500-ing the page; supports.frontend stays >=0.1.55. Core 0.1.31 is the DB-driven public routing + CMS-in-CMS app builder wave (issue #30): public page URLs are now resolved from a new `page_routes` table via the open-access endpoint GET /cms-api/v1/pages/resolve (controller PageController::resolvePublicPath; the page still applies full ACL/publish/preview/data-access, returning 404 on unauthorized), the page-content response gains route metadata fields (route_params, matched_url_pattern, canonical_url), dynamic URL segments are exposed to interpolation as {{route.<snake_case_param>}} and folded into the page cache key, the seeded reset/validate flows become parameterized routes (/reset + /reset/{user_id}/{token}, /validate/{user_id}/{token}), pages gain a `page_surface` (public|cms) lookup for the CMS-in-CMS split, and four admin routes are added (POST /admin/pages/export, GET /admin/pages/{id}/export/suggest, POST /admin/pages/import/validate, POST /admin/pages/import - the portable page-bundle export/import) plus POST /admin/pages/cms-app (the list+detail wizard). The frontend switches its entire public routing to /pages/resolve and reads route_params (reset/validate styles), so this is a coordinated wave: supports.frontend is raised 0.1.55 -> 0.1.57 in lockstep (frontend 0.1.57 adopts /pages/resolve + the export/import + wizard UI and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.31). Pairs with @selfhelp/shared >=1.18.0 (the route-metadata types + PAGES.RESOLVE endpoint) and mobile 0.1.31 (resolve-by-path deep linking). pluginApiVersion is unchanged (0.1.0): no plugin API surface changed. Core 0.1.33 is the navigation-overhaul wave (strict nav contract v2, issue-free destructive cleanup pre-release): migration Version20260706074503 adds navigation_menu_items.layer (top header row for web_header roots), drops the dead id_child_source/auto_include_depth columns + the navigationChildSources lookup type, carries config.footer_layout into id_preset (columns/inline join navigationMenuPresets) and drops navigation_menus.config (the translation columns description/aria_label ship since Version20260702164932; the legacy n key existed only in bundle v1.0). GET /cms-api/v1/navigation now emits the strict always-present item shape (id/item_type/label/description/aria_label/icon/mobile_icon/position/layer/external_url/page/is_active/children) plus menu-level preset/max_depth/item_limit — config is gone — and the admin navigation payloads mirror it (layer on create/update/reorder, footer preset on menu update, translations with label/description/aria_label). The navigation bundle becomes selfhelp/navigation-bundle v2.0 (layer + aria_label keys, no config, no n); v1.0 bundles are rejected with a re-export error. That is a BREAKING response/request-shape change the frontend consumes, so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.58 -> 0.1.59 (frontend 0.1.59 adopts the strict payload + layered header/footer-preset rendering + bundle v2.0 and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.33). The same 0.1.33 wave adds the child-page navigation presentation contract (migration Version20260706143547: navigationChildrenNavModes lookups sidebar/pills/none, menu-level children_nav default + show_breadcrumbs, per-item children_nav override, emitted on web menus + admin payloads + bundle v2.0), the all-language content search dedupe, and the admin pages title/titles fields - all consumed by the same frontend 0.1.59, so no extra floor moves. Pairs with @selfhelp/shared 1.21.5 (wave previously staged as shared 2.0.0). pluginApiVersion is unchanged (0.1.0): no plugin API surface changed. Core 0.1.34 is the first-class cms_apps product unit (migration Version20260708140000): cms_apps table + page assignment roles, admin.cms_app.* permissions, /admin/cms-apps CRUD+scaffold, removal of POST /admin/pages/cms-app. That is a BREAKING admin API change the frontend consumes, so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.59 -> 0.1.60 (frontend 0.1.60 adopts CMS Apps admin UI and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.34). Core 0.1.35 restores field-based entry-list/entry-record binding (breaking), adds DataTableFilterService + POST /admin/data/query-preview, bundle import relink for entry-table fields_map, and SectionAccessibleRouteService so interpolation + query-preview share page-read-filtered route placeholders. Frontend 0.1.62 adopts the column mapper, fields-map editors, and filter preview UI, so supports.frontend is raised 0.1.60 -> 0.1.62. Core 0.1.36 switches entry-record to load_record_from (same as entry-record-form), collapses the feature-branch WIP migrations into five finals, and pairs with frontend 0.1.63 + @selfhelp/shared 1.21.6 (the navigation/CMS-apps/entry-binding wave previously staged as shared 2.0.0–3.0.1 was republished as 1.21.5). supports.frontend is raised 0.1.62 -> 0.1.63. Do not treat shared 2.x/3.x as the current pin; active pin is 1.21.7 (1.21.6 is immutable on npm; should_fallback typing requires 1.21.7).
